# Tech Lead Notes

> Multi-source, three-tier mirror of CC-licensed project-management resources. Tier 1 = agent index (compact catalog); Tier 2 = distilled per-concept cards (TL;DR / core ideas / anti-patterns / cross-refs); Tier 3 = full original text per source. License per source — see each chunk's `license:` frontmatter field.

- [Tech Lead Notes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/index.md): Operational playbook for tech leads — a multi-source, three-tier mirror of CC-licensed project-management, Scrum, and engineering- effectiveness resources, split into navigable chunks with YAML frontmatter so humans,…

## Tier 1 — Agent index

- [Agent index](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/agent-index.md): one-line catalog of every concept, principle, performance domain and methodology covered. Designed to fit in a system prompt; links out to Tier-2 cards.

## Tier 2 — Distilled cards

### Distilled — overview, glossary, cross-references

- [Distilled (Tier 2)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/index.md): This is a dense, agent-friendly distillation of Nader K. Rad's PMBOK Guide: Underneath the Surface (CC-BY-4.0). Anecdotes, historical commentary and personal asides are removed. Each card is a self-contained brief with…
- [About the distilled tier](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/about.md): The Tier-2 distilled cards (everything under docs/distilled/) are editorial summaries of the ten CC-licensed sources listed in docs/sources.md. The full original text is preserved in Tier 3 (docs/sources/<source-id>/…)…
- [Cross-references](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/cross-references.md): A topic-to-source map. Use these clusters when you want to gather all the cards related to a single concept.
- [Glossary](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/glossary.md): Distilled definitions of recurring terms, derived from the book. Each entry links to the primary distilled card or full chunk that develops it.

### Distilled — PMBOK 7 principles

- [PMBOK 7 principles (distilled)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/pmbok-7/index.md): Twelve principles that PMBOK 7 uses as a lens to interpret project management situations. They are not a methodology and not a checklist of activities — apply them to evaluate decisions and tailor methods.
- [Stewardship](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/pmbok-7/01-stewardship.md): TL;DR: A project manager is a facilitator and steward of people and resources, not a technical decision-maker or a "boss".
- [Collaborative team environment](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/pmbok-7/02-collaborative-team.md): TL;DR: A great project needs a great team, not just great individuals. Build the team through real work, not corporate rituals.
- [Engage stakeholders](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/pmbok-7/03-engage-stakeholders.md): TL;DR: A stakeholder is anyone with interest who can impact the project (not just the customer). Identify them aggressively, communicate often, and tailor the channel per group.
- [Focus on value](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/pmbok-7/04-focus-on-value.md): TL;DR: Value is the benefits-to-cost ratio (not "benefits" alone). Every project decision must be traceable to why the project exists. Value lives at the portfolio layer; projects align to it and feed it information.
- [System interactions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/pmbok-7/05-system-interactions.md): TL;DR: Decisions ripple through interconnected systems and are distorted by cognitive biases. Reason holistically and apply critical thinking, not gut feel.
- [Leadership behaviors](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/pmbok-7/06-leadership.md): TL;DR: Leadership is influencing others toward desired outcomes — a behavior, not a job title. Anyone can lead; PMs should both be leaders and find and empower hidden leaders.
- [Tailor based on context](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/pmbok-7/07-tailor.md): TL;DR: No universal PM system fits every project. Tailor a chosen methodology to your size/culture/project — but tailoring is system design, not cherry-picking.
- [Build quality in](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/pmbok-7/08-quality.md): TL;DR: Quality is prevention, integrated into the system, not final inspection bolted on. It applies to the product and to the PM system itself. Investment must be net-positive; if not, the quality system is poorly…
- [Optimize risk responses](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/pmbok-7/09-risk.md): TL;DR: A risk is any uncertainty (positive or negative) that can impact the project. Manage risks proactively through layered responses, continuously, with cost-justified investments.
- [Navigate complexity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/pmbok-7/10-complexity.md): TL;DR: Project complexity comes from three sources: people, non-linear interactions, and ambiguity/uncertainty. There is no distinct technique here — applying the other principles is how you navigate complexity.
- [Adaptability and resiliency](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/pmbok-7/11-adaptability.md): TL;DR: Predictive vs adaptive (Agile) approaches each fit a different kind of product. PMBOK 7 is approach-agnostic for the product, but the PM system itself must always be adaptive because it deals with people.
- [Enable change to achieve the envisioned future state](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/pmbok-7/12-enable-change.md): TL;DR: Distinguish output (what the project builds) from outcome (what changes in the world). Outputs only matter when they cause adoption and outcomes; outcome management is largely program management, but the project…

### Distilled — NUPP principles

- [NUPP principles (distilled)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/nupp/index.md): NUPP = Nearly Universal Principles of Projects. Open non-proprietary resource (<https://nupp.guide>) — usable as a lens to re-interpret any other PM system. Six principles, intentionally generic and short.
- [Prefer results and the truth to affiliations](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/nupp/01-results-over-affiliations.md): TL;DR: Don't be tribal about a methodology. Use what works for the project; learn from systems you don't even use.
- [Preserve and optimize energy and resources](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/nupp/02-preserve-energy.md): TL;DR: Project resources and the PM's decision-making energy are both finite. Optimize for high-leverage work and protect mental bandwidth.
- [Always be proactive](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/nupp/03-always-be-proactive.md): TL;DR: Reactive behavior is the default. Project work consistently benefits from proactive action — reach out before problems escalate.
- [A chain is only as strong as its weakest link](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/nupp/04-weakest-link.md): TL;DR: All PM domains interact. Over-investing in one (typically scheduling) while neglecting others ruins the lot.
- [Don't do anything without a clear purpose](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/nupp/05-clear-purpose.md): TL;DR: Every practice/artifact must have a purpose you can name. Otherwise it's cargo-cult work — looks like methodology, produces nothing.
- [Use repeatable elements](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/nupp/06-repeatable-elements.md): TL;DR: Ad-hoc work is expensive and error-prone. Build checklists and recurring cycles so cognition can go to the actual work.

### Distilled — PRINCE2 principles

- [PRINCE2 principles (distilled)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/prince2/index.md): Seven principles that support the PRINCE2 methodology. Mostly applicable to all projects, but designed as a coherent set with the PRINCE2 method (covered separately in Methodologies).
- [Continued business justification](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/prince2/01-continued-business-justification.md): TL;DR: Re-check the project's business case throughout — at predefined gates. Things change; sunk cost is not a reason to continue.
- [Learn from experience](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/prince2/02-learn-from-experience.md): TL;DR: Reusing existing PM systems and prior-project lessons is cheaper than reinventing them. Both between and within projects.
- [Defined roles and responsibilities](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/prince2/03-roles.md): TL;DR: Beyond very small projects, people need explicit clarity on what's expected of them — and what they can expect from others.
- [Manage by stages](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/prince2/04-manage-by-stages.md): TL;DR: You can only plan in detail for a short horizon. Use a high-level plan up-front and a detailed plan only for the next stage.
- [Manage by exception](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/prince2/05-manage-by-exception.md): TL;DR: Delegate decisions within tolerances; escalate only when thresholds are breached. Saves senior energy and creates buy-in.
- [Focus on products](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/prince2/06-focus-on-products.md): TL;DR: Plan around the products (deliverables), not around activities. Activity-first planning prematurely commits to one solution.
- [Tailor to suit the project](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/prince2/07-tailor-to-project.md): TL;DR: PRINCE2 (and almost every PM system) cannot be used out-of-the-box. Tailor up-front and keep adapting throughout.

### Distilled — performance domains

- [Performance domains (distilled)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/performance-domains/index.md): Eight domains PMBOK 7 uses to enrich any chosen methodology. They are sets of concerns to verify your methodology covers adequately for your project. If your method is thin in a domain, extend it (don't replace it).
- [Stakeholders (performance domain)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/performance-domains/01-stakeholders.md): TL;DR: Five-step continuous loop — Identify → Understand & analyze → Prioritize → Engage → Monitor — covering both internal and external stakeholders. Modern approaches balance both; Agile-only or "external-only" focus…
- [Team (performance domain)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/performance-domains/02-team.md): TL;DR: Three concerns: team structure, team building, and leadership. Two motivations: ethical (people deserve a safe healthy environment regardless of project gain) and instrumental (better team → better project).
- [Development approach and life cycle (performance domain)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/performance-domains/03-development-approach.md): TL;DR: Predictive vs adaptive is decided by the product, not by team preference. "Hybrid" is mostly a misnomer; only valid form is multiple parallel projects with different approaches per independent product part.…
- [Planning (performance domain)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/performance-domains/04-planning.md): TL;DR: All projects plan — adaptive ones plan differently, not less. Plan continually, cover all 10 knowledge areas, separate plan (artifacts) from meta-plan (how-to-plan).
- [Project Work (performance domain)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/performance-domains/05-project-work.md): TL;DR: Plan first, then work — but with a constant two-way dance between plans and reality. Capture follow-up items as they emerge; never let them slip.
- [Measurement (performance domain)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/performance-domains/06-measurement.md): TL;DR: Measure to direct work and to evaluate it. Use forecast to completion as the headline metric. What you measure becomes the goal — choose proxies that align with real value.
- [Delivery (performance domain)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/performance-domains/07-delivery.md): TL;DR: Requirements → Deliverables → Activities is the correct order in both predictive and adaptive projects. Limit WIP. Verify each deliverable before delivery; never bet on a single big-bang final delivery.
- [Uncertainty (performance domain)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/performance-domains/08-uncertainty.md): TL;DR: Risk management is the dominant topic. Choose the sophistication that matches the project; default to simpler. Sensitive risks belong at the portfolio level, not project.

### Distilled — methodologies

- [Methodologies (distilled)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/methodologies/index.md): The PMBOK Guide is not a methodology — it interprets and enriches one. This section summarizes the four methodologies the book covers as worked examples.
- [Methodology comparison](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/methodologies/comparison.md): Side-by-side decision aid based on the four methodologies the book covers. None is universally right; pick by context. Use PMBOK 7's principles + performance domains across whichever you choose.
- [DSDM®](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/methodologies/dsdm.md): TL;DR: First-generation Agile method designed from scratch for large, multi-team IT development. Structurally inspired by PRINCE2. Centralized PM, timeboxed delivery, MoSCoW prioritization.
- [P3.express](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/methodologies/p3-express.md): TL;DR: Open, minimalist, general-purpose method with monthly + weekly + daily cycles. Use as-is, tailor gradually. Out of scope: micro-projects (use micro.P3.express) and mega-projects (heavy tailoring or another…
- [PRINCE2®](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/methodologies/prince2.md): TL;DR: Sophisticated, maximalist, general-purpose, predictive methodology. Out-of-the-box defaults target large/super-large projects; must be tailored before use. Three management layers, seven principles, two-stage…
- [Scrum](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/methodologies/scrum.md): TL;DR: First-generation Agile framework for IT development, single team ≤10 people. Decentralized PM (no PM role). Covers a subset of project management — no initiation, no closure. Lightweight; very heavy tailoring is…

### Distilled — Scrum Guide 2020

- [Scrum Guide 2020 (distilled)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/index.md): Cards distilled from the canonical 2020 Scrum Guide by Ken Schwaber and Jeff Sutherland, focused on what an agent or practitioner needs: prescriptions, accountabilities, anti-patterns and cross-refs. The Scrum Guide…
- [Scrum definition](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/01-scrum-definition.md): TL;DR: Scrum is a lightweight framework for delivering value on complex problems through small cross-functional teams, iterative Sprints, and continuous inspection-and-adaptation. Purposefully incomplete — defines just…
- [Scrum theory and the three pillars](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/02-theory-and-pillars.md): TL;DR: Scrum is grounded in empiricism (knowledge from experience and observation) and lean thinking (reduce waste, keep the essentials). Empiricism is operationalized through three pillars — Transparency, Inspection,…
- [Scrum Values](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/03-scrum-values.md): TL;DR: Five values that make Scrum's empiricism work in practice: Commitment · Focus · Openness · Respect · Courage. Without them the three pillars collapse and Scrum becomes ceremony.
- [Scrum Team — structure](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/04-scrum-team.md): TL;DR: A single small team (≤10), three accountabilities (Developers, Product Owner, Scrum Master), no sub-teams or hierarchies, focused on one Product Goal at a time. Cross-functional and self-managing; the entire…
- [Developers (Scrum role)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/05-developers.md): TL;DR: Anyone in the Scrum Team committed to creating any aspect of a usable Increment each Sprint. Inclusive label — "Developer" is not limited to programmers.
- [Product Owner](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/06-product-owner.md): TL;DR: The Product Owner is accountable for maximizing product value and for effective Product Backlog management. One person, not a committee. May delegate, but accountability stays.
- [Scrum Master](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/07-scrum-master.md): TL;DR: The Scrum Master is accountable for establishing Scrum as defined in the Guide and for the Scrum Team's effectiveness. A true leader who serves — the team, the Product Owner, and the wider organization.
- [Scrum events — overview](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/08-events-overview.md): TL;DR: Five events. The Sprint is a container for the other four (Sprint Planning, Daily Scrum, Sprint Review, Sprint Retrospective). Each event is a formal opportunity to inspect and adapt an artifact. Skipping or…
- [The Sprint](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/09-the-sprint.md): TL;DR: Fixed-length timebox of one month or less that contains all Scrum work. Heartbeat of Scrum — turns ideas into value. New Sprint starts immediately after the previous one ends.
- [Sprint Planning](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/10-sprint-planning.md): TL;DR: Initiates the Sprint by laying out the work. Whole-team collaboration. Output = Sprint Goal + Sprint Backlog. Timebox 8 h for a 1-month Sprint.
- [Daily Scrum](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/11-daily-scrum.md): TL;DR: 15-minute event for Developers. Inspect progress toward the Sprint Goal; produce an actionable plan for the next day.
- [Sprint Review](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/12-sprint-review.md): TL;DR: Inspect the outcome of the Sprint with stakeholders and decide what to do next. Working session, not a demo. Timebox 4 h for a one-month Sprint.
- [Sprint Retrospective](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/13-sprint-retrospective.md): TL;DR: Plan ways to increase quality and effectiveness. Final event of the Sprint. Timebox 3 h for a one-month Sprint.
- [Product Backlog (+ Product Goal)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/14-product-backlog.md): TL;DR: Single source of work undertaken by the Scrum Team — emergent, ordered list. Its commitment is the Product Goal: the long-term objective the team plans against.
- [Sprint Backlog (+ Sprint Goal)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/15-sprint-backlog.md): TL;DR: A plan by and for the Developers. Equation: Sprint Goal (why) + selected Product Backlog items (what) + plan to deliver (how). Its commitment is the Sprint Goal.
- [Increment (+ Definition of Done)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/16-increment.md): TL;DR: A concrete, usable, additive stepping stone toward the Product Goal. Its commitment is the Definition of Done: the formal description of "Done" quality. Multiple Increments may be created within a Sprint and…

### Distilled — Scrum Guide Expansion Pack (v2026.1)

- [Scrum Guide Expansion Pack (v2026.1) — distilled](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/index.md): Cards distilled from the Scrum Guide Expansion Pack (v2026.1) by Ralph Jocham, John Coleman and Jeff Sutherland — an explicit adaptation of the 2020 Scrum Guide that adds theory, expands roles, and refines artifacts…
- [Purpose and key departures from SG2020](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/01-purpose-and-departures.md): TL;DR: The Expansion Pack (SGEP) is not the immutable Scrum Guide. It explains the what and why "for the current times", evolves regularly, and explicitly departs from SG2020 in several places — those departures are…
- [Foundational theory (SGE)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/02-foundational-theory.md): TL;DR: SGE explicitly grounds Scrum in seven theoretical foundations: complexity, emergence, self-managing teams, professionalism, lean thinking, empiricism, cadence. SG2020 only named empiricism + lean.
- [Additional theory (SGE)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/03-additional-theory.md): TL;DR: SGE adds six more theoretical lenses beyond the foundational seven: product thinking, systems thinking, discovery, leadership, first-principles thinking, people and change. Together with the foundational theory…
- [Stakeholder (SGE — 4th role)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/04-stakeholder.md): TL;DR: SGE elevates Stakeholder to a named role alongside PO, Product Developer, and Scrum Master. Stakeholders are not in the Scrum Team and have no accountabilities, but their classification, engagement, and…
- [Supporter (SGE — new concept)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/05-supporter.md): TL;DR: Supporter is a Stakeholder subtype — a change agent who actively makes the organization coherent with Scrum. SGE-specific concept, not present in SG2020.
- [Artificial Intelligence (SGE — new participant)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/06-artificial-intelligence.md): TL;DR: AI may be a Product Developer or a tool, but human accountability stays. Use the "human in the loop" model. AI supports Scrum's empirical process — does not override it or ethical decision-making.
- [Product Developer (SGE)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/07-product-developer.md): TL;DR: SGE-equivalent of SG2020 "Developers". May be human or automated. At least one Product Developer must be human. Accountabilities are extended.
- [Product Owner — SGE extensions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/08-product-owner.md): TL;DR: SGE keeps SG2020 PO accountabilities and extends substantially: PO is a leader for the Product, must be human, maximizes long-term value, owns the Definition of Outcome Done, and trusts Product Developers rather…
- [Scrum Master — SGE extensions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/09-scrum-master.md): TL;DR: SGE recasts the Scrum Master as a change agent at all organizational levels who is accountable for the effectiveness of the Scrum Team, Stakeholders, Supporters and affected people. Must be human. Anti-pattern…
- [Product (artifact) and Product Vision (SGE)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/10-product-and-vision.md): TL;DR: SGE introduces Product as a separate artifact distinct from the Increment, and adds Product Vision as an optional but supported long-term concept. SG2020 had neither explicitly.
- [Definition of Outcome Done (SGE — new commitment)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/11-definition-of-outcome-done.md): TL;DR: New commitment in SGE, attached to the Product (not the Increment). Describes observable evidence measures that demonstrate realized benefits = "value validation". Pairs with the renamed Definition of Output Done.
- [Definition of Output Done (renamed DoD)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/12-definition-of-output-done.md): TL;DR: SGE renames the SG2020 Definition of Done to Definition of Output Done to disambiguate from outcome (the new Definition of Outcome Done). Attached to the Increment. Quality measures expressing due diligence…
- [Product Backlog extensions (PBI, Acceptance/Outcome Criteria, Refinement)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/13-product-backlog-extensions.md): TL;DR: SGE keeps the SG2020 Product Backlog basics and adds explicit treatment of Product Backlog Item (PBI), Acceptance Criteria + Outcome Criteria, Refinement (which can be a formal event), and richer Product Goal…
- [Sprint Backlog and Sprint Goal — SGE extensions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/14-sprint-backlog-extensions.md): TL;DR: Sprint Backlog still by-and-for the Product Developers. Major change: the Sprint Goal commitment is now owned by the whole Scrum Team (SG2020 had "Developers"). SGE re-introduces the "start with one or two PBIs"…
- [Events — SGE extensions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/15-events-extensions.md): TL;DR: SGE preserves the five SG2020 events. The notable extensions: Refinement may become a formal event, Sprint Planning's three sub-topics are explicitly framed as Why / What / How, and event semantics are extended…

### Distilled — DORA Metrics in Jira

- [DORA Metrics in Jira (distilled)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/dora-metrics-jira/index.md): Cards distilled from a self-authored methodology document on applying DORA (DevOps Research and Assessment) metrics in Jira + GitHub stacks. Practical, opinionated, end-to-end: methodology → ticket discipline →…
- [DORA fundamentals](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/dora-metrics-jira/01-dora-fundamentals.md): TL;DR: DORA defines four metrics correlating with org-level software performance. Read them together — Velocity ones (Deployment Frequency, Lead Time) without Stability ones (CFR, MTTR) are misleading. Out-of-the-box…
- [Ticket discipline (developer + tech lead + DoD)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/dora-metrics-jira/02-ticket-discipline.md): TL;DR: Discipline is the prerequisite for trustworthy DORA metrics. Four general principles, strict naming/sizing for developers, oversight rules for tech leads, and a production- gated Definition of Done.
- [GitHub-side enforcement (integration, hooks, Actions, OSS tools)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/dora-metrics-jira/03-github-side.md): TL;DR: Three maturity levels — basic integration → local hooks → server-side GitHub Actions. Hooks alone are bypassable; GitHub Actions are the authoritative gate. For most teams, environment: production in a GitHub…
- [Jira setup checklist](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/dora-metrics-jira/04-jira-setup-checklist.md): TL;DR: End-to-end actionable checklist for getting DORA metrics working in Jira Premium with GitHub. Ten sections from administrative setup through ongoing monitoring. Verification expected 2-3 weeks after setup.
- [Anti-patterns and common pitfalls](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/dora-metrics-jira/05-anti-patterns.md): TL;DR: Diagnostic table mapping symptom → likely cause → solution for the most common DORA-in-Jira failures. Plus the cross-cutting "do-nots" extracted from developer and tech-lead discipline.

### Distilled — P3.express manual (v2)

- [P3.express manual (v2) — distilled](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/p3-express/index.md): Cards distilled from the canonical P3.express manual v2 — an open, minimalist project-management methodology built to comply with the NUPP principles. 33 management activities in 7 groups; 4 documents; designed to be…
- [P3.express cycle and activity codes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/p3-express/01-cycle-and-activities.md): TL;DR: 33 activities in 7 groups. The project has a monthly cycle (B+E groups around continuous weekly C and daily D management), bracketed by Project Initiation (A) at the start and Project Closure (F) + Post-Project…
- [P3.express roles](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/p3-express/02-roles.md): TL;DR: Five named roles span two layers: management team (Sponsor + Project Manager + Customer PM if external) and production teams (Team Leader for internal, Supplier PM for external). The perspective rule — "you are…
- [P3.express artifacts (4 documents)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/p3-express/03-artifacts.md): TL;DR: Just four documents. Combined Follow-Up Register collapses what other methodologies split into 3-5 logs. Every deliverable and every follow-up item gets a custodian — a named person responsible until closure.
- [Peer review and go/no-go gates](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/p3-express/04-peer-review-and-go-no-go.md): TL;DR: P3.express formalizes two recurring patterns: an external peer review of the management work (A07, B03, F03) and a go/no-go decision by the sponsor at each major boundary (A08, B04). They are deliberately…
- [Focused communication](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/p3-express/05-focused-communication.md): TL;DR: P3.express ends almost every cycle with a focused communication — a defined message to a defined audience at a defined moment. The pattern is so universal in the manual that it appears as A10, B05, C04, E03, and…

### Distilled — micro.P3.express manual

- [micro.P3.express manual — distilled](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/micro-p3-express/index.md): Cards distilled from the micro.P3.express manual — a flavor of P3.express for micro-projects with 1-7 team members.
- [micro.P3.express overview and deltas vs P3.express](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/micro-p3-express/01-overview-and-deltas.md): TL;DR: Designed for 1-7 team members. Replaces P3.express's monthly cycle with a weekly-only cadence. Replaces named roles with hats that one person can swap. Tooling/repository setup is explicit (A3). Use it for tiny…
- [Hats, not roles](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/micro-p3-express/02-hats-not-roles.md): TL;DR: micro.P3.express replaces P3.express's named roles with four hats, each owning a set of concerns. Hats don't grant authority — they assign responsibility for making sure those concerns aren't neglected. One…

### Distilled — The Kanban Guide (v2025.5)

- [The Kanban Guide — distilled](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/kanban-guide/index.md): Cards distilled from The Kanban Guide v2025.5 — the canonical, minimal definition of Kanban as a flow-optimization strategy. Kanban is not a methodology or a "Sprint replacement"; it is a strategy applied to whatever…
- [Kanban — three practices and the Definition of Workflow](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/kanban-guide/01-three-practices-and-dow.md): TL;DR: Kanban is three practices working in tandem — Define and visualize the workflow, Actively manage items, Improve the workflow. The shared explicit understanding of what flows through the system is the Definition…
- [Kanban — flow metrics (the four mandatory)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/kanban-guide/02-flow-metrics.md): TL;DR: Kanban requires exactly four flow metrics; everything else is optional and context-specific. The four feed the three practices — they're how you tell whether your DoW is delivering effectiveness, efficiency and…
- [Kanban — when to use (and when not)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/kanban-guide/03-when-to-use.md): TL;DR: Kanban applies wherever value flows through a workflow — knowledge-work industry doesn't matter. The decision is rarely "Scrum or Kanban"; it's "what do we want from flow?". Use Kanban when iteration cadence is…

### Distilled — Open Guide to Kanban (v2025.7)

- [Open Guide to Kanban — distilled](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/open-guide-to-kanban/index.md): Cards distilled from The Open Guide to Kanban v2025.7 — a community-expanded adaptation of The Kanban Guide. The Open Guide preserves the original strictly and adds material in italics: more definitions, an expanded…
- [Open Guide to Kanban — deltas from The Kanban Guide](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/open-guide-to-kanban/01-deltas-from-kanban-guide.md): TL;DR: The Open Guide preserves The Kanban Guide verbatim (kept in regular type) and adds material in italics. Five categories of additions: (1) expanded metrics catalog, (2) Outcomes/Impact/Value (OIV) extension, (3)…

### Distilled — Wardley Maps (Simon Wardley)

- [Wardley Maps — distilled cards](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/wardley-maps/index.md): Dense per-concept cards distilled from Simon Wardley's book Wardley Maps (CC BY-SA 4.0). Use these for fast topical lookup; use the Tier-3 chapters for verbatim text and figures.
- [Wardley Maps — anatomy of a map](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/wardley-maps/01-anatomy-of-a-map.md): TL;DR: A Wardley Map is a value chain plotted against an evolution axis. It is anchored on user needs at the top, decomposed into the components required to serve them, and each component is positioned horizontally by…
- [Wardley Maps — evolution stages](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/wardley-maps/02-evolution-stages.md): TL;DR: Every component on a map sits in one of four evolution stages — genesis → custom-built → product (incl. rental) → commodity (incl. utility). The stage tells you how to treat it: build, buy, outsource, replace.…
- [Wardley Maps — doctrine (universal practices)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/wardley-maps/03-doctrine.md): TL;DR: Doctrine = the universal practices that apply to any organization regardless of context. Apply them before context-specific gameplay. Wardley groups doctrine into phases (communication, development, operation,…
- [Wardley Maps — climatic patterns](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/wardley-maps/04-climatic-patterns.md): TL;DR: Climate = the rules that act on every map regardless of who you are or what you build. They aren't your choices; they happen to you. Map them before deciding any move. Headline rules: everything evolves, change…
- [Wardley Maps — gameplay](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/wardley-maps/05-gameplay.md): TL;DR: Gameplay = the deliberate moves you make on the map to advance your purpose. Unlike doctrine (universal), gameplay is context-specific — what works here may fail there. Wardley catalogs ~60 plays grouped by…
- [Wardley Maps — Pioneers, Settlers, Town Planners](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/wardley-maps/06-pioneers-settlers-town-planners.md): TL;DR: Different evolution stages need fundamentally different attitudes in the people working on them. Pioneers explore the unknown (genesis/custom). Settlers turn prototypes into robust products (product). Town…
- [Wardley Maps — strategy cycle and when to use](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/wardley-maps/07-strategy-cycle-and-when-to-use.md): TL;DR: Wardley's strategy cycle is an OODA-like loop: purpose → landscape → climate → doctrine → leadership (act), then back. Strategy is iterative, not a one-shot plan. Wardley Mapping is the right tool when you need…

### Distilled — Synthesis (cross-source)

- [Synthesis cards (cross-source)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/synthesis/index.md): These cards combine material from multiple sources in this repository. Where individual per-source cards answer "what does framework X say about Y?", synthesis cards answer *"what does the whole corpus say about Y, and…
- [Done", quality, and peer review across frameworks](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/synthesis/01-done-and-quality.md): TL;DR: Every framework defines "done" as a commitment that prevents premature closure. They agree on the principle (built-in, not inspected; enforced by visible criteria; evolves over time) but differ in what level the…
- [Stakeholders and supporters across frameworks](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/synthesis/02-stakeholders-cross-source.md): TL;DR: All frameworks agree a stakeholder is anyone with interest who can impact the project (not just the customer). They diverge on structure: PMBOK 7 treats stakeholders as a performance domain with a 5-step…
- [Output vs Outcome and value validation across frameworks](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/synthesis/03-outcome-vs-output.md): TL;DR: Output = what the project builds. Outcome = what changes in the world for stakeholders. Most failures look like "successful delivery" while the outcome doesn't materialize. PMBOK 7 introduces the distinction;…
- [Measurement, metrics, and Parkinson's law across frameworks](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/synthesis/04-measurement-and-metrics.md): TL;DR: Two laws govern all metrics work: (1) what you measure becomes the goal (Parkinson / Goodhart), and (2) metrics must be read together, not in isolation (DORA). Forecast to completion is the only effective…
- [Risk, uncertainty, incidents, and stability across frameworks](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/synthesis/05-risk-and-stability.md): TL;DR: All frameworks treat risk as uncertainty that can impact the project (positive or negative). They differ on operationalization: PMBOK 7 prescribes layered cost-justified responses; SGE adds proactive risk…
- [Tailoring and methodology selection across frameworks](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/synthesis/06-tailoring-and-method-selection.md): TL;DR: Every framework explicitly addresses tailoring, and they converge on don't cherry-pick ("Frankenstein"). They diverge on when to tailor (PRINCE2: upfront required; P3.express: gradual only) and on immutability…
- [Roles and accountabilities across frameworks](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/synthesis/07-roles-across-frameworks.md): TL;DR: Same project responsibilities are split across wildly different role names across frameworks. The mapping table below collapses the noise. Three universal patterns: a business authority (Sponsor/Executive/PO), a…
- [Uncertainty domains and method fit](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/synthesis/08-uncertainty-domains.md): TL;DR: The corpus does not name "Cynefin" but builds the same distinction. SGE explicitly flags four spaces — ordered, complex, chaotic, plus the implied complicated — and warns that **the space shifts over time, and…

## Tier 3 — Full original text (per source / chapter)

### Source: DORA Metrics in Jira: Methodology, Rules, and Setup

#### (landing)

- [DORA Metrics in Jira: Methodology, Rules, and Setup](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/dora-metrics-jira/index.md): Self-authored methodology document on using DORA (DevOps Research and Assessment) metrics in Jira for teams on the Jira + GitHub stack. Covers methodology, ticket discipline, GitHub-side enforcement, and Jira setup.

#### 1. Introduction: Methodology and Indicators

- [1. Introduction: Methodology and Indicators](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/dora-metrics-jira/01-1-introduction-methodology-and-indicators.md): DORA (DevOps Research and Assessment) is a Google Cloud research group that defined four key metrics correlating with overall organizational performance in software engineering. These metrics have become the industry…

#### 2. Ticket Discipline Rules

- [2. Ticket Discipline Rules](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/dora-metrics-jira/02-2-ticket-discipline-rules.md): 2. Size matters. Ten half-day tickets beat one week-long ticket. 3. A ticket is alive until the code is in production. Don't close before actual deployment. 4. Workflow needs automation — manual status drag-and-drop…

#### 3. Managing Metrics from the GitHub Side

- [3. Managing Metrics from the GitHub Side](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/dora-metrics-jira/03-3-managing-metrics-from-the-github-side.md): Three levels of maturity, from minimal to full control.

#### 4. Jira Setup Checklist

- [4. Jira Setup Checklist](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/dora-metrics-jira/04-4-jira-setup-checklist.md): Create the following automation rules (Project settings → Automation):

#### Appendix: Common Pitfalls and How to Avoid Them

- [Appendix: Common Pitfalls and How to Avoid Them](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/dora-metrics-jira/05-appendix-common-pitfalls-and-how-to-avoid-them.md): ---

### Source: The Kanban Guide (v2025.5 (May 2025))

#### (landing)

- [The Kanban Guide (v2025.5 (May 2025))](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/kanban-guide/index.md): Canonical, minimal Kanban reference. © 2019-2025 Orderly Disruption Limited, Daniel S. Vacanti, Inc.. Licensed under CC BY-SA 4.0.

#### Preface

- [Preface](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/kanban-guide/01-preface.md): May 2025

#### Definition of Kanban

- [Definition of Kanban](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/kanban-guide/02-definition-of-kanban.md): Kanban is a strategy for optimizing the flow of value through a process. It comprises the following three practices working in tandem:

#### Why Use Kanban?

- [Why Use Kanban?](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/kanban-guide/03-why-use-kanban.md): Central to the definition of Kanban is the concept of flow. Flow is the movement of potential value through a system. As most workflows exist to optimize value, the strategy of Kanban is to optimize value by optimizing…

#### Kanban Theory

- [Kanban Theory](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/kanban-guide/04-kanban-theory.md): Kanban draws on established flow theory, including but not limited to systems thinking, lean principles, queuing theory (batch size and queue size), variation, and quality control. Continually improving a Kanban system…

#### Kanban Practices

- [Kanban Practices](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/kanban-guide/05-kanban-practices.md): Optimizing flow requires defining what flow means in a given context. The explicit shared understanding of flow among Kanban system members within their context is called a Definition of Workflow (DoW). DoW is a…

#### Flow Metrics

- [Flow Metrics](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/kanban-guide/06-flow-metrics.md): The application of Kanban requires collecting and analyzing a minimum set of flow metrics. They reflect the Kanban system’s current health and performance and will help inform decisions about how value is delivered.…

#### Endnote

- [Endnote](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/kanban-guide/07-endnote.md): One can and likely should add other principles, methodologies, and techniques to the Kanban system. Still, the minimum set of practices, metrics, and the spirit of optimizing value must be preserved.

#### History of Kanban

- [History of Kanban](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/kanban-guide/08-history-of-kanban.md): The present state of Kanban can be traced to the Toyota Production System (and its antecedents) and the work of people like Taiichi Ohno and W. Edwards Deming. The collective set of practices for knowledge work, now…

#### Acknowledgments

- [Acknowledgments](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/kanban-guide/09-acknowledgments.md): In addition to all who helped to develop Kanban over the years, we would like to thank the following individuals specifically for their contributions to this guide:

#### License

- [License](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/kanban-guide/10-license.md): This work is licensed by Orderly Disruption Limited and Daniel S. Vacanti, Inc. under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License.

### Source: micro.P3.express manual

#### (landing)

- [micro.P3.express manual](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/index.md): micro.P3.express is a flavor of P3.express designed for micro-projects with approximately 1 to 7 team members, which can be run in various environments, from mega- to micro‑organizations (including single-person ones).…

#### Project Initiation

- [Group A — Project Initiation](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/01-a-project-initiation/index.md): See individual activity pages.
- [A1 — Identify the high-level decision maker(s)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/01-a-project-initiation/01-identify-the-high-level-decision-maker-s.md): This is the first management activity in the Project Initiation activity group, which is a group for creating a foundation for the project and deciding whether you will execute it.
- [A2 — Understand and distribute the hats](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/01-a-project-initiation/02-understand-and-distribute-the-hats.md): You don’t want the project work to be done by sending each specialist task to a department and them assigning it to their experts on an ad hoc basis. Instead, the necessary experts must be officially appointed as…
- [A3 — Select tools and create a project repository](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/01-a-project-initiation/03-select-tools-and-create-a-project-repository.md): At this point, we need to set up the tools and a well-formed repository for storing the management and production documents of the project.
- [A4 — Create a common understanding](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/01-a-project-initiation/04-create-a-common-understanding.md): The person wearing the Project Manager hat helps all the team members to collaborate and reach a common understanding of the project. This understanding will serve as the foundation of future efforts and as a…
- [A5 — Have Project Initiation peer-reviewed](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/01-a-project-initiation/05-have-project-initiation-peer-reviewed.md): A good Project Manager should always have a critical perspective:
- [A6 — Make a go/no-go decision](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/01-a-project-initiation/06-make-a-go-no-go-decision.md): At this point, we’re almost ready for the go/no-go decision. Before asking the responsible person or group to make the decision, each hat-wearer should express their concerns:
- [A7 — Conduct a focused communication](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/01-a-project-initiation/07-conduct-a-focused-communication.md): This is the last management activity in the Project Initiation group, required when the organization is larger than the project team.

#### Weekly Initiation

- [Group C — Weekly Initiation](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/02-c-weekly-initiation/index.md): See individual activity pages.
- [C1 — Revise and refine the common understanding](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/02-c-weekly-initiation/01-revise-and-refine-the-common-understanding.md): This is the first management activity in the Weekly Initiation group.
- [C2 — Have the Weekly Initiation peer-reviewed](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/02-c-weekly-initiation/02-have-the-weekly-initiation-peer-reviewed.md): It’s important to always remain critical:
- [C3 — Make a go/no-go decision](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/02-c-weekly-initiation/03-make-a-go-no-go-decision.md): It’s time for the cyclic go/no-go decision. Before making the decision, each hat-wearer expresses their concerns:
- [C4 — Conduct a focused communication](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/02-c-weekly-initiation/04-conduct-a-focused-communication.md): This is the end of the Weekly Initiation activity group. If the organization is larger than the project team, a focused communication may be necessary.

#### Daily Management

- [Group D — Daily Management](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/03-d-daily-management/index.md): See individual activity pages.
- [D1 — Manage follow-up items](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/03-d-daily-management/01-manage-follow-up-items.md): All team members should be continuously looking for issues, risks, changes, and improvement ideas.
- [D2 — Close completed deliverables](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/03-d-daily-management/02-close-completed-deliverables.md): Custodians add comments to their deliverable cards on the Integrated Project Board, and eventually, move them to the “to-review” column when they are completed. Then, it’s time to make sure each deliverable is really…

#### Weekly Closure

- [Group E — Weekly Closure](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/04-e-weekly-closure/index.md): See individual activity pages.
- [E1 — Measure and report performance](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/04-e-weekly-closure/01-measure-and-report-performance.md): This is the first management activity in the Weekly Closure group.
- [E2 — Evaluate stakeholder satisfaction](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/04-e-weekly-closure/02-evaluate-stakeholder-satisfaction.md): We need to have an implicit or explicit evaluation of the satisfaction of team members and external stakeholders.
- [E3 — Capture lessons and plan for improvements](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/04-e-weekly-closure/03-capture-lessons-and-plan-for-improvements.md): It’s a good idea to pause, reflect on the previous week, and see what you’ve learned from it that can help you have a better project next week.
- [E4 — Consider swapping hats for the week](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/04-e-weekly-closure/04-consider-swapping-hats-for-the-week.md): This is the end of the Weekly Closure activity group, and we have one more thing to do.

#### Project Closure

- [Group F — Project Closure](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/05-f-project-closure/index.md): See individual activity pages.
- [F1 — Double-check and hand over the final output](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/05-f-project-closure/01-double-check-and-hand-over-the-final-output.md): The first thing we need to do in the Project Closure activity group is double-check to ensure the final output of the project is complete and matches the expectations.
- [F2 — Evaluate stakeholder satisfaction](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/05-f-project-closure/02-evaluate-stakeholder-satisfaction.md): E2 provides continual evaluation of stakeholder satisfaction, but each of those evaluations is mainly focused on one cycle of the project. Therefore, we need to have an evaluation at the end as well to understand the…
- [F3 — Have the Project Closure peer-reviewed](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/05-f-project-closure/03-have-the-project-closure-peer-reviewed.md): As usual, it’s important to remain open and critical:
- [F4 — Consider swapping hats for Post-Project Management](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/05-f-project-closure/04-consider-swapping-hats-for-post-project-management.md): If the organization is larger than the project team, a different group of people (e.g., the portfolio management team) may be responsible for Post-Project Management. If that’s not the case, the team members will stay…
- [F5 — Archive the project documents](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/05-f-project-closure/05-archive-the-project-documents.md): The management and production documents generated in the project will be helpful in similar projects you may have in the future. For this reason, it’s important to make sure they stay accessible.
- [F6 — Celebrate](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/05-f-project-closure/06-celebrate.md): At this point, you’re done with the project and it’s closed, which is a good time to celebrate!
- [F7 — Conduct a focused communication](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/05-f-project-closure/07-conduct-a-focused-communication.md): This is the last activity in Project Closure, required when the organization is larger than the project team.

#### Post-Project Management

- [Group G — Post-Project Management](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/06-g-post-project-management/index.md): See individual activity pages.
- [G1 — Evaluate the benefits](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/06-g-post-project-management/01-evaluate-the-benefits.md): The Post-Project cycle usually repeats every 1 to 6 months for 1 to 5 years, depending on the type of project. In this cycle, we evaluate the benefits of the project’s output to take action and examine our original…
- [G2 — Generate new ideas](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/06-g-post-project-management/02-generate-new-ideas.md): Based on G1, you know how the project’s output has performed. Now you can act on that information.
- [G3 — Conduct a focused communication](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/micro-p3-express/06-g-post-project-management/03-conduct-a-focused-communication.md): If there are stakeholders other than the hats involved in the post-project management cycle, we need to inform them of the results.

### Source: NUPP — Nearly Universal Principles of Projects

#### (landing)

- [NUPP — Nearly Universal Principles of Projects](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/nupp/index.md): NUPP is a collection of nearly universal principles of projects: those we’d do well to follow in all projects, regardless of the methodologies and approaches that we use, to maximize our success.
- [NUP1 — Prefer results and the truth to affiliations](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/nupp/01-nup1-prefer-results-and-the-truth-to-affiliations.md): We all have a natural tendency to belong to groups, a tendency that often goes beyond its basic form, creates strong affiliations, and causes problems. We lose a lot more than we gain because of affiliations. We can…
- [NUP2 — Preserve and optimize energy and resources](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/nupp/02-nup2-preserve-and-optimize-energy-and-resources.md): Resources are limited. Resources available to the project are limited, as is the mental energy you have to make good decisions. You should preserve and optimize this resource for yourself and for the project, and help…
- [NUP3 — Always be proactive](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/nupp/03-nup3-always-be-proactive.md): There’s a natural tendency in us to be reactive. It can help us preserve our energy dealing unimportant matters, or it may give us better results when we are dealing with something in which we’re completely…
- [NUP4 — A chain is only as strong as its weakest link](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/nupp/04-nup4-a-chain-is-only-as-strong-as-its-weakest-link.md): There are various domains in projects, and they all need attention; we must have a holistic perspective of the project. Paying attention to a seemingly important domain (e.g., time) is not enough, because all domains…
- [NUP5 — Don't do anything without a clear purpose](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/nupp/05-nup5-don-t-do-anything-without-a-clear-purpose.md): You shouldn’t do anything unless it has a clear purpose. Imagine two parallel worlds where everything is the same except for the thing that you’re considering doing: How different would those worlds be? Is the…
- [NUP6 — Use repeatable elements](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/nupp/06-nup6-use-repeatable-elements.md): An ad hoc approach to the project takes too much energy and resources, and always runs the risk of missing some of the necessary elements. The best way of simplifying what has to be done is to use repeatable elements,…

### Source: Open Guide to Kanban (v2025.7 (July 2025))

#### (landing)

- [Open Guide to Kanban (v2025.7 (July 2025))](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/open-guide-to-kanban/index.md): Adaptation of The Kanban Guide for hybrid / knowledge-work contexts. © 2019-2025 Orderly Disruption Limited, Daniel S. Vacanti, Inc.. Licensed under CC BY-SA 4.0.

#### Preface

- [Preface](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/open-guide-to-kanban/01-preface.md): This document aims to provide open and adaptable guidance for Kanban and Flow, drawing on ideas curated from various communities. It aims to serve as a coherent reference for various communities in addition to their…

#### Definition of Kanban in the Context of Knowledge Work

- [Definition of Kanban in the Context of Knowledge Work](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/open-guide-to-kanban/02-definition-of-kanban-in-the-context-of-knowledge-work.md): Kanban is a strategy for optimizing the Flow of Value through a system. It is a signaling system to call for Work or inventory. It comprises the following three practices working in tandem:

#### Why Use Kanban?

- [Why Use Kanban?](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/open-guide-to-kanban/03-why-use-kanban.md): Central to the understanding of Kanban is the concept of Flow. In a Kanban system, Flow is the movement of Value through that Kanban system. As most Kanban workflows exist to optimize Value, the strategy of Kanban is…

#### Kanban Theory

- [Kanban Theory](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/open-guide-to-kanban/04-kanban-theory.md): The Kanban system draws on various approaches and understanding including, but not limited to, systems thinking (5), lean principles (4), queuing theory (batch size (6-7) and queue size (1,13-14)), variation (2,11),…

#### Kanban Practices

- [Kanban Practices](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/open-guide-to-kanban/05-kanban-practices.md): Optimizing Flow requires defining what Flow of Value means in a given context, the (ideally) smooth movement and delivery of potential or (ideally) realized benefits for Stakeholders. The explicit shared understanding…

#### Inform Flow Optimization with Appropriate Measures or Metrics

- [Inform Flow Optimization with Appropriate Measures or Metrics](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/open-guide-to-kanban/06-inform-flow-optimization-with-appropriate-measures-or-metrics.md): The Flow metrics and measures apply to the appropriate ‘started’ and ‘finished’ points established by the Kanban system members in their Definition of Workflow. If there are multiple sets of ‘started’ and ‘finished’…

#### Endnote

- [Endnote](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/open-guide-to-kanban/07-endnote.md): Only the Kanban Practices, the minimum criteria Definition of Workflow, and a selection of metrics or measures are mandatory; everything else is optional. Consider the context. Kanban system members should foster the…

#### History of Kanban

- [History of Kanban](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/open-guide-to-kanban/08-history-of-kanban.md): The present state of Kanban can be traced to the Toyota Production System (and its antecedents) and the work of people such as Taiichi Ohno (9). The collective set of practices for Knowledge Work, now commonly referred…

- [Acknowledgments](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/open-guide-to-kanban/09-acknowledgments.md): People acknowledged here do not necessarily agree with what is written in this document, and that is ok. Nevertheless, the Open Guide to Kanban owes a massive debt of gratitude to:

#### Appendix

- [Appendix](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/open-guide-to-kanban/10-appendix.md): Control of ‘Started but not Finished Work’, also referred to as WIP control, can be represented on a Kanban board in any way that Kanban system members deem appropriate, including, but not limited to, painter’s tape…

- [References](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/open-guide-to-kanban/11-references.md): References are placed here to inform readers of opportunities for further study. They do not necessarily support the text in this guide:

### Source: P3.express manual (v2)

#### (landing)

- [P3.express manual (v2)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/index.md): P3.express uses a simple process, as shown in the diagram above. This process consists of 33 management activities in 7 groups. Click on any of the activities in the diagram to open its description, or simply start…

#### Project Initiation

- [Group A — Project Initiation](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/01-a-project-initiation/index.md): See individual activity pages.
- [A01 — Appoint the sponsor](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/01-a-project-initiation/01-appoint-the-sponsor.md): This management activity belongs to the Project Initiation group. This group of activities are run at the beginning to prepare us for the project.
- [A02 — Appoint the project manager](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/01-a-project-initiation/02-appoint-the-project-manager.md): This management activity belongs to the Project Initiation group. This group of activities are run at the beginning to prepare us for the project.
- [A03 — Appoint key team members](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/01-a-project-initiation/03-appoint-key-team-members.md): This management activity belongs to the Project Initiation group. This group of activities are run at the beginning to prepare us for the project.
- [A04 — Describe the project](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/01-a-project-initiation/04-describe-the-project.md): This management activity belongs to the Project Initiation group. This group of activities are run at the beginning to prepare us for the project.
- [A05 — Identify and plan deliverables](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/01-a-project-initiation/05-identify-and-plan-deliverables.md): This management activity belongs to the Project Initiation group. This group of activities are run at the beginning to prepare us for the project.
- [A06 — Identify risks and plan responses](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/01-a-project-initiation/06-identify-risks-and-plan-responses.md): This management activity belongs to the Project Initiation group. This group of activities are run at the beginning to prepare us for the project.
- [A07 — Peer-review project initiation](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/01-a-project-initiation/07-peer-review-project-initiation.md): This management activity belongs to the Project Initiation group. This group of activities are run at the beginning to prepare us for the project.
- [A08 — Make a go/no-go decision](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/01-a-project-initiation/08-make-a-go-no-go-decision.md): This management activity belongs to the Project Initiation group. This group of activities are run at the beginning to prepare us for the project.
- [A09 — Kick off the project](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/01-a-project-initiation/09-kick-off-the-project.md): This management activity belongs to the Project Initiation group. This group of activities are run at the beginning to prepare us for the project.
- [A10 — Conduct a focused communication](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/01-a-project-initiation/10-conduct-a-focused-communication.md): This management activity belongs to the Project Initiation group. This group of activities are run at the beginning to prepare us for the project.

#### Monthly Initiation

- [Group B — Monthly Initiation](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/02-b-monthly-initiation/index.md): See individual activity pages.
- [B01 — Have the team revise the plans](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/02-b-monthly-initiation/01-have-the-team-revise-the-plans.md): This management activity belongs to the Monthly Initiation group. This group of activities are done in the beginning of each month to prepare us for a new monthly cycle.
- [B02 — Revise the project description](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/02-b-monthly-initiation/02-revise-the-project-description.md): This management activity belongs to the Monthly Initiation group. This group of activities are done in the beginning of each month to prepare us for a new monthly cycle.
- [B03 — Have Monthly Initiation peer-reviewed](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/02-b-monthly-initiation/03-have-monthly-initiation-peer-reviewed.md): This management activity belongs to the Monthly Initiation group. This group of activities are done in the beginning of each month to prepare us for a new monthly cycle.
- [B04 — Make a go/no-go decision](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/02-b-monthly-initiation/04-make-a-go-no-go-decision.md): This management activity belongs to the Monthly Initiation group. This group of activities are done in the beginning of each month to prepare us for a new monthly cycle.
- [B05 — Conduct a focused communication](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/02-b-monthly-initiation/05-conduct-a-focused-communication.md): This management activity belongs to the Monthly Initiation group. This group of activities are done in the beginning of each month to prepare us for a new monthly cycle.

#### Weekly Management

- [Group C — Weekly Management](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/03-c-weekly-management/index.md): See individual activity pages.
- [C01 — Measure performance](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/03-c-weekly-management/01-measure-performance.md): This management activity belongs to the Weekly Management group, which is a group of activities done at the beginning of each week.
- [C02 — Plan responses to deviations](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/03-c-weekly-management/02-plan-responses-to-deviations.md): This management activity belongs to the Weekly Management group, which is a group of activities done at the beginning of each week.
- [C03 — Identify and respond to new risks](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/03-c-weekly-management/03-identify-and-respond-to-new-risks.md): This management activity belongs to the Weekly Management group, which is a group of activities done at the beginning of each week.
- [C04 — Conduct a focused communication](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/03-c-weekly-management/04-conduct-a-focused-communication.md): This management activity belongs to the Weekly Management group, which is a group of activities done at the beginning of each week.

- [D01 — Manage follow-up items](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/04-d-daily-management/01-manage-follow-up-items.md): This management activity belongs to the Daily Management activity group: It’s done daily.
- [D02 — Accept completed deliverables](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/04-d-daily-management/02-accept-completed-deliverables.md): This management activity belongs to the Daily Management activity group: It’s done daily.

#### Monthly Closure

- [Group E — Monthly Closure](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/05-e-monthly-closure/index.md): See individual activity pages.
- [E01 — Evaluate stakeholder satisfaction](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/05-e-monthly-closure/01-evaluate-stakeholder-satisfaction.md): This management activity belongs to the Monthly Closure group. This group of activities are done at the end of each month to close the cycle and prepare us for the next one.
- [E02 — Plan improvements](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/05-e-monthly-closure/02-plan-improvements.md): This management activity belongs to the Monthly Closure group. This group of activities are done at the end of each month to close the cycle and prepare us for the next one.
- [E03 — Conduct a focused communication](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/05-e-monthly-closure/03-conduct-a-focused-communication.md): This management activity belongs to the Monthly Closure group. This group of activities are done at the end of each month to close the cycle and prepare us for the next one.

#### Project Closure

- [Group F — Project Closure](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/06-f-project-closure/index.md): See individual activity pages.
- [F01 — Hand over the product](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/06-f-project-closure/01-hand-over-the-product.md): This management activity belongs to the Project Closure group. This activity group is done when the product of the project is complete, or when you decide to cancel the project.
- [F02 — Evaluate stakeholder satisfaction](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/06-f-project-closure/02-evaluate-stakeholder-satisfaction.md): This management activity belongs to the Project Closure group. This activity group is done when the product of the project is complete, or when you decide to cancel the project.
- [F03 — Have Project Closure peer-reviewed](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/06-f-project-closure/03-have-project-closure-peer-reviewed.md): This management activity belongs to the Project Closure group. This activity group is done when the product of the project is complete, or when you decide to cancel the project.
- [F04 — Archive project documents](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/06-f-project-closure/04-archive-project-documents.md): This management activity belongs to the Project Closure group. This activity group is done when the product of the project is complete, or when you decide to cancel the project.
- [F05 — Re-assign the team](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/06-f-project-closure/05-re-assign-the-team.md): This management activity belongs to the Project Closure group. This activity group is done when the product of the project is complete, or when you decide to cancel the project.
- [F06 — Celebrate](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/06-f-project-closure/06-celebrate.md): This management activity belongs to the Project Closure group. This activity group is done when the product of the project is complete, or when you decide to cancel the project.

#### Post-Project Management

- [Group G — Post-Project Management](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/p3-express/07-g-post-project-management/index.md): See individual activity pages.
